D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Cross-Cultural Colour Meaning — 6 × 6 Grid

White = death (East Asia) vs purity (West); red = wedding (China) vs warning (US); green = Islam vs envy — colour meaning is partly biology, partly history

Aslam (2006) surveyed 35 countries on colour-meaning associations. Universals exist (red = arousal, blue = trust), but specific symbolic loadings vary. Marketing crosses these lines — yellow packaging works in Russia (sun + warmth), reads as cheap in the US.

Pick a region to highlight its meaning row.
